name: Vigo – Río Eifonso trail and ascent of Alto de CUVI
name_original: Vigo – sendero del Río Eifonso y subida de Alto de CUVI
region_intro: Hiking around Vigo offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to forested hills and island trails. The region is characterized by its Atlantic coastline, including the Cíes Islands, and a belt of forest parks surrounding the city. Rivers and streams flow through the terrain, leading to beaches and providing varied natural environments for exploration. Best hiking trails around Vigo The most popular hiking route is Isla de Cíes – Sud trail, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route explores the southern part of the Cíes Islands, known for their natural environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monteagudo Route, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail offers views across the Cíes Islands. Local hikers also love the Vigo – Río Eifonso trail and ascent of Alto de CUVI, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through river valleys and ascending to Alto de CUVI, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Vigo is defined by coastal paths, forest parks, and island trails. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Vigo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Vigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vigo?
A: Vigo offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 160 trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy coastal walks to more challenging ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Vigo?
A: Yes, Vigo has numerous easy trails perfect for beginners or families. Many of the city's forest parks, such as those in Saiáns and Bembrive, offer accessible paths and picnic areas. Coastal trails like parts of the Senda Azul also provide gentle walks with beautiful sea views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls near Vigo?
A: Yes, the region around Vigo features several trails that lead to beautiful waterfalls. You can explore routes that pass by highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2836141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza de Camos&lt;/a&gt;. For a longer adventure, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38201466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Waterfall in the Forest – O Xunqueiro Beach loop from Coruxo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more challenging route but rewards with natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Vigo?
A: Yes, Vigo offers several circular routes for hikers who prefer to end their journey where they started. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Causeway of O Vao – Peito Lighthouse loop from Parque Nacional Marítimo Terrestre das Illas Atlánticas de Galicia&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a scenic loop experience.

Q: What are the best places to find panoramic views while hiking in Vigo?
A: For stunning panoramic views, consider hiking up Monte Castro, which offers vistas of the Vigo estuary, its harbor, and the Cíes Islands. Another excellent viewpoint is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Paralaia&lt;/a&gt;, or the famous 'El Mejor Banco del Mundo' (The Best Bench in the World) for breathtaking perspectives of the Vigo Bridge and its estuary.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking trails around Vigo?
A: Many of the natural areas and forest parks around Vigo are suitable for hiking with dogs, provided they are kept under control. Always check local regulations for specific trails, especially within protected areas like the Cíes Islands, where access for pets might be restricted.

Q: How can I reach hiking trails in Vigo using public transport?
A: Vigo's urban and peri-urban bus network provides access to many trailheads, especially those within the city's forest parks and coastal areas. For trails further afield, such as those leading to the Cíes Islands, you would typically need to combine public transport with a ferry service. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and ferry timetables in advance.

Q: Where can I park my car when going for a hike in Vigo?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For urban trails like Monte Castro, public parking lots are usually available nearby. For trails in forest parks or coastal areas, designated parking spaces can often be found at trail entrances or nearby villages. Always look for official parking areas to avoid inconvenience.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I explore on hikes in Vigo?
A: Vigo's hiking trails offer diverse natural attractions. You can explore the unique ecosystems of the Cíes Islands, discover historical sites like the Iberian settlement on Monte Castro, or enjoy the tranquility of river valleys on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991745194/vigo-rio-eifonso-trail-and-ascent-of-alto-de-cuvi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo – Río Eifonso trail&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features numerous beaches, viewpoints, and forest landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Vigo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal paths and island trails to serene forest parks, and the rewarding panoramic views from various viewpoints.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Vigo?
A: While many trails are moderate, experienced hikers can find more challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991745194/vigo-rio-eifonso-trail-and-ascent-of-alto-de-cuvi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo – Río Eifonso trail and ascent of Alto de CUVI&lt;/a&gt;, for example, involves a significant elevation gain. The region also includes parts of the coastal variant of the Portuguese St. James Way, offering longer, more demanding routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vigo?
A: The best time to hike in Vigo is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be warm, especially in July and August, but coastal and island trails often benefit from sea breezes. Winters are typically mild but can be rainy, so appropriate gear is recommended.
tips: The path of the Beade Forest Park goes up to the University of Vigo
The trail passes through the Botanical Park where it meets the San Cibran Chapel
Old stone buildings of the water mill along the river. These were used to turn sweet chestnuts into flour by the locals before the introduction of electricity.
The path of the Eifonso river...trails, waterfalls, mills and even a beautiful waterfall.
